governance_contract: Manufacturer design intent determines public family and detailed category. Board DNA describes behaviour and may refine recommendation ranking, but it must not infer or override the board's fundamental identity.
editorial_rules: A board is classified by the manufacturer's intended role, not by how frequently an owner may ride it.
Daily Driver is never a fallback for unknown or ambiguous records.
Performance twins are assigned model by model: fish-derived designs remain Fish, versatile hybrids may be Daily Driver, and narrow quality-wave designs are Performance Shortboards.
Board DNA augments family and category; it does not create them.
Low-confidence records remain review-required and must not be promoted by inference.

model_overrides.reason: High-volume performance step-up intended for larger and stronger surf; it is not a fish.
Scaled Driver lineage for larger surfers; the design remains a performance shortboard rather than a fish.
Stepped-down Driver design for low-end high-performance surfing, not an all-round hybrid.
Elite-team Driver development establishes a competition performance role.
Elite-team Driver development establishes a competition performance role.
Competition-focused small-wave shortboard; not a generic daily driver.
Performance-oriented symmetrical alternative design; it is not fish-first.
Retro performance daily-driver role does not meet Step Up intent.
Longer RNF 96 expression remains fish-led despite its mid-length rail line.
Modern performance fish; public and detailed labels must not contradict each other.
Modern small-wave twin derived from established fish design lineage.
Quality-wave performance design; everyday usage does not make it a Daily Driver.
Broader-range daily driver with a performance edge.
Weak-wave speed and forgiving small-wave performance are the core role.
Versatile hybrid shortboard, not a fish.
High-performance role remains primary despite improved accessibility.

review_required.reason: Current governed source record lacks a detailed manufacturer description.
